flags: contrast text contains typo: both sentences show 見える
cases.notes: Sentences are genuinely natural with appropriate register matching, and the 見る/見える/見せる confusion family is tight and well-distinguished. The core_meaning builds real usage intuition. Only flaw: the confused_with contrast mistakenly writes 「富士山が見える」for both the wrong and correct example, blunting the intended minimal pair.

Sentences are genuinely natural with appropriate register matching (casual よ/よね/んだ, formal ません), and collocations like 〜そうに見える are idiomatic. The confusion set (見る/見せる) is the tight, correct family of confusables, and the core_meaning crisply conveys the spontaneous-visibility intuition rather than a flat gloss.

Sentences are natural and varied across registers, each matching its label well (the casual 見せてよ, the keigo お見せしてもよろしいでしょうか, the literary そぶりも見せずに). The 見る/見せる/見える confusion family is exactly the right tight set, with crisp use_when distinctions, and the core_meaning builds genuine usage intuition around intentional showing vs. spontaneous visibility.

flags: last sentence uses ~てみる, not core 見る meaning
cases.notes: Example sentences are natural and well-matched to their registers, with good use of casual ellipsis (映画見たよ). The 見る/見える/見せる confusion family is tight and well-distinguished. The core_meaning is a somewhat circular dictionary-style restatement (目を使って見る時) that builds little intuition, and the final sentence drifts to the ~てみる grammar rather than illustrating the verb itself.
